## Design question
The whole abstract adjunction mate-calculus for the D2′ unit square is already CLOSED axiom-clean.
What remains is ONE concrete identity living on the **presheaf↔sheaf `.val` boundary**, and five
prover iterations have failed to close it — NOT because a lemma is missing, but because of pervasive
Lean tactic friction manipulating `SheafOfModules`/`PresheafOfModules` `.val` composites. I need the
**Mathlib idiom** for three pieces of `.val`-boundary bookkeeping (below), and above all: **is there
a canonical Mathlib idiom — a monoidal-functor coherence/`app`/`ext` lemma — that discharges this
whole concrete identity sectionwise in one shot, instead of the manual associativity chase that has
cost 5 iters?** "The named lemmas exist, PROCEED" is NOT the answer I need (the prior eta247 consult
already certified the abstract glue and the route still didn't close). I need the *shape of the
proof that avoids the friction*.

Concretely, after the closed telescope, the remaining goal `(∗∗)` is the presheaf-level identity:
```
(presheafAdj.unit.app 𝟙ᵖ ≫ (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ').map (toSheafify_Y (F 𝟙ᵖ)))
   ≫ R_X.map ((SheafOfModules.pushforward φ).map (a_Y.map (η F) ≫ sheafifyUnitIso.hom))
 = R_X.map (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ)
```
where `R_X = SheafOfModules.forget X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)`,
`φ = f.toRingCatSheafHom`, `φ' = φ.hom`, `F = PresheafOfModules.pullback φ'`, `η F` its
OplaxMonoidal unit, `a_Y = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 Y.ringCatSheaf.val)`.

The three sub-pieces (documented in-code at the `sorry`):

**(i) `SheafOfModules.pushforward` vs `PresheafOfModules.pushforward` on morphisms, through `.val`.**
Is `R_X.map ((SheafOfModules.pushforward φ).map g) = (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ').map g.val`
a *named* Mathlib identity, a `simp` normal form, or only a `rfl` defeq one must coax? What is the
canonical Mathlib lemma relating `(SheafOfModules.pushforward φ).map g` to its underlying
presheaf-morphism `((…).map g).val` / `(PresheafOfModules.pushforward …).map g.val`? (Look in
`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Pushforward` and the `SheafOfModules`/`PresheafOfModules`
`forget`/`toPresheaf`/`.val` API.)

**(ii) The Y-side sheafification right-triangle in split `.val`/composite-functor form.**
Need `toSheafify_Y (F 𝟙ᵖ) ≫ (a_Y.map (η F)).val ≫ (sheafifyUnitIso.hom).val = η F`, i.e. the
sheafification unit/counit right-triangle on the SHEAF `𝒪_Y` pushed to underlying presheaves. What is
the exact Mathlib lemma (`Adjunction.right_triangle_components` / `PresheafOfModules.sheafification…`
toSheafify naturality) and — crucially — the idiom to state/apply it that does NOT trip the
`Category.assoc` silent-match failure on these composites (see Failed approaches)?

**(iii) STEP 7 — the presheaf lax-monoidal unit `ε` reconciliation (the SOLE genuinely-new math item).**
Need `Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ') = (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ).val`
checked sectionwise (both act on sections as `φ.hom.app X`). What is the canonical Mathlib
characterization of `Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε` for `PresheafOfModules.pushforward`? How is the
lax-monoidal structure on `PresheafOfModules.pushforward` *defined* (so its `ε` has a computable
`app`)? Is there an `ε_app` / `pushforward_ε` lemma, or must it be unfolded through
`restrictScalars`/`Functor.LaxMonoidal` field accessors? NOTE: there is **NO** `Functor.LaxMonoidal`
instance on the *sheaf* `SheafOfModules.pushforward` — step 7 must live at the presheaf level. The
prior eta247 analogy (line 40) wrongly assumed a *sheaf-level* `ε`-by-`rfl`; that is exactly the
hole step 7 fills, so do not reuse that claim.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.

52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |

70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).

81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.

They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.

91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.

102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.

106	The user provides hints in two places:

108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. 14	`IsIso (a_Y.map (Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ')))`

26	## Decisions identified
27	
28	### Decision: do the four named glue lemmas exist with usable signatures?
29	
30	- **Mathlib idiom**: all three categorical glue lemmas exist verbatim in
31	  `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.Unique`:
32	  - `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.homEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app` —
33	    `(adj1.homEquiv x (F'.obj x)) ((adj1.leftAdjointUniq adj2).hom.app x) = adj2.unit.app x`.
34	  - `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app` —
35	    `adj1.unit.app x ≫ G.map ((adj1.leftAdjointUniq adj2).hom.app x) = adj2.unit.app x`.
36	  - `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit` —
37	    `(adj1.leftAdjointUniq adj2).hom.app (G.obj x) ≫ adj2.counit.app x = adj1.counit.app x`.
38	  Re-association variants also present: `unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_assoc`,
39	  `leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit_assoc`, `leftAdjointUniq_trans_app_assoc` (same module).

46	### Decision: is the two-level leftAdjointUniq mate chase closeable with standard API, or is a coherence lemma missing?
47	
48	- **Mathlib idiom**: the reduction lemmas the prover transposes through all exist:
49	  - `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.homEquiv_unit` / `homEquiv_counit`
50	    (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.Basic`).
51	  - `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_η`
52	    (`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Functor`):
53	    `Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η F = (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm (Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε G)` — this is the
54	    lemma that turns the η-bridge into the pushforward-side ε identity, i.e. it is *why* the mate
55	    chase is the canonical route.
56	  Plus `NatTrans` naturality and the project's own
57	  `pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit`.
58	- **Project's current path**: the η-bridge is the UNIT-side mirror of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`
59	  (L904–990), which closed the COUNIT/δ-side mate transport axiom-clean in the same file using

# Analogy: closing the D2′ `(∗∗)` presheaf↔sheaf `.val`-boundary identity — Mathlib idiom for (i)/(ii)/(iii) and the one-shot question

## Mode
api-alignment

## Slug
eps250

## Iteration
250

## Question
After the closed abstract mate-calculus telescope, one concrete presheaf-level identity `(∗∗)`
remains on the `SheafOfModules`/`PresheafOfModules` `.val` boundary. Five iters failed it on Lean
tactic friction (not a missing lemma). What is the Mathlib idiom for the three sub-pieces
(i) pushforward-through-`.val`, (ii) the Y-side sheafification right-triangle, (iii) the presheaf
lax-unit `ε` reconciliation — and, above all, is there a single monoidal-functor coherence/`app`/`ext`
lemma that discharges the whole identity sectionwise in one shot, sidestepping the assoc chase?

## Project artifact(s)
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:1648–1741 — `pullbackEtaUnitSquare`, closed
  telescope + the open `(∗∗)` `sorry` with the 3-substep in-code recipe.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:1502–1516 — `presheafUnit_comp_map_eta`
  (CLOSED), `:= Adjunction.unit_app_unit_comp_map_η`.
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`:1108–1118 — `presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal`
  (the `ε` whose section value step iii needs).
- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/PresheafInternalHom.lean`:290–327 —
  `restrictScalarsLaxε` / `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal` (defines the `ε` sectionwise).

## Headline answer to the one-shot question

**There is NO single sectionwise `ext`/`app`/coherence lemma that closes all of `(∗∗)` at once, and
the project should stop hunting for one.** The obstruction is intrinsic, not a missing lemma: the LHS
contains the *sheafification unit* `presheafAdj.unit.app 𝟙ᵖ` (= `CategoryTheory.toSheafify`, the
plus-construction map). `toSheafify.app X` is opaque — it does NOT compute on sections — so
`ext X; simp [app-lemmas]` cannot evaluate the LHS. Any route that survives must keep the unit
*abstract* until it is folded away.

The lemma that does fold it away **already exists and is already in hand**: the genuine "one-shot
coherence lemma" is `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.unit_app_unit_comp_map_η`
(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Functor.lean:969`),
`adj.unit.app (𝟙_ C) ≫ G.map (η F) = ε G`, re-exported by the project as the CLOSED
`presheafUnit_comp_map_eta` (TensorObjSubstrate.lean:1502). It absorbs the opaque-unit part in one
move. What it does NOT also absorb is (i) the `.val`/pushforward reshaping and (ii) the
sheafification right-triangle that bring the LHS into the literal shape
`unit.app 𝟙ᵖ ≫ (pushforward φ').map (η F)`; those are unavoidable bookkeeping. Only *after* that
collapse does the goal become `ε(pushforward φ') = (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ).val`, which IS a
clean one-shot sectionwise `ext` (both sides `= φ.hom.app X`). So the correct shape is hybrid:
**abstract reshaping (i)+(ii) → the existing coherence lemma → sectionwise `ext` (iii)** — exactly
the 3-step recipe already in the code, with the friction-defeating idioms below.

## Decisions identified

### Decision (i): `SheafOfModules.pushforward` on morphisms through `R_X = forget ⋙ restrictScalars(𝟙)`

- **Mathlib idiom**: it is a **named `@[simp]` lemma**, not a defeq one must coax.
  `SheafOfModules.pushforward` is `@[simps map_val]`
  (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean:43`), generating
  `SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val : ((pushforward φ).map f).val = (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ.hom).map f.val`
  (verified via loogle, exact signature). Combine with `SheafOfModules.forget` `@[simps]`
  (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf.lean:66`) ⇒ `forget_map : (forget R).map φ = φ.val`,
  `SheafOfModules.comp_val` (`Sheaf.lean:60`, `@[simp, reassoc]`, `(f ≫ g).val = f.val ≫ g.val`),
  and the `restrictScalars (𝟙)`-is-identity defeq (`restrictScalarsCompToPresheaf (𝟙) = Iso.refl`,
  `Presheaf/ChangeOfRings.lean:71`; project memory `restrictscalars-id-defeq`). Net rewrite:
  `R_X.map ((pushforward φ).map g) = (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ').map g.val` holds by
  `simp only [Functor.comp_map, SheafOfModules.forget_map, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val]`
  with the `restrictScalars(𝟙)` step absorbed defeq.
- **Gap**: identical — the named simp lemma exists. No rfl-coax needed.
- **Verdict**: PROCEED.

### Decision (ii): Y-side sheafification right-triangle in split `.val`/composite-functor form

- **Mathlib idiom**: it is `unit-naturality ⋙ right-triangle`, two standard lemmas.
  `toSheafify_Y (F 𝟙ᵖ) ≫ (a_Y.map (η F)).val` is the *unit naturality* of the presheaf
  sheafification adjunction at `η F`: the unit's underlying-presheaf component is `toSheafify`
  (`PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf_map_sheafificationAdjunction_unit_app`,
  `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Sheafification.lean:144`), so
  `(sheafificationAdjunction _).unit.naturality (η F)` gives
  `toSheafify (F 𝟙ᵖ) ≫ (a_Y.map (η F)).val = η F ≫ toSheafify 𝟙ᵖ_Y`. Then `≫ (sheafifyUnitIso.hom).val`
  is the **right triangle on the sheaf `𝒪_Y`**: `sheafifyUnitIso = (asIso counit).app (unit Y)` and
  `𝟙ᵖ_Y = (unit Y).val`, so `toSheafify 𝟙ᵖ_Y ≫ (sheafifyUnitIso.hom).val = 𝟙` by
  `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.right_triangle_components`. Net: the composite `= η F`.
- **Friction & idiom**: this needs ONE reassociation `t ≫ (A ≫ B) = (t ≫ A) ≫ B`, which is exactly
  where `rw [Category.assoc]` silently fails (the `≫` carries an implicit ring-presheaf argument that
  is `X.ringCatSheaf.val` vs `X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ …` — defeq, not syntactic, so `rw`'s motive
  fails to unify). **Two robust idioms**: (a) reuse the project's proven X-triangle shape
  `(Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans (h ▸ Category.id_comp _)` (it already works for `hXtri` at
  L1714); or (b) **`simp only [Category.assoc]`** rather than `rw` — simp matches up to reducible
  defeq and reassociates where `rw` cannot. Best structural fix: open with a single
  `show … = …` that restates the goal in the syntactic `PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ …)`
  category (since `X.ringCatSheaf.val` is `rfl`-equal to it), after which plain `rw [Category.assoc]`
  works for the rest of the block.
- **Gap**: identical — both lemmas exist; reuse the L1714 shape (do NOT re-derive a new assoc path).
- **Verdict**: PROCEED.

### Decision (iii): STEP 7 — `ε(PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ') = (unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ).val`

- **Mathlib characterization of the `ε`**: `PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ' = pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ'`
  (`Presheaf/Pushforward.lean:86`). For a composite, `Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp` sets
  `ε := ε G ≫ G.map (ε F)` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Functor.lean:222`), here
  `G = restrictScalars φ'`, `F = pushforward₀`. Since `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat`'s monoidal has
  `εIso := Iso.refl` (`Presheaf/PushforwardZeroMonoidal.lean:35`), `ε F = 𝟙`, so
  `ε(pushforward φ') = ε(restrictScalars φ')` (the second factor is `G.map 𝟙 = 𝟙`). The project's
  `restrictScalarsLaxε` (PresheafInternalHom.lean:290) gives the section value directly:
  `ε(restrictScalars φ').app X = Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ'.app X).hom)`,
  and `ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Adjunction.lean:106`,
  `@[simp]`) gives `ε(restrictScalars f) r = f r`. So `ε(pushforward φ').app X` acts as `φ'.app X = φ.hom.app X`.
- **RHS section value**: `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply`
  (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/PullbackFree.lean:71`, `:= rfl`):
  `(unitToPushforwardObjUnit φ).val.app X a = φ.hom.app X a`. And `R_X.map upu` is defeq `upu.val`
  (the `restrictScalars(𝟙)`/`forget` collapse of (i)).
- **Idiom**: a clean sectionwise `ext` — `apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext; intro X`
  (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf.lean:100`), then `ModuleCat.hom_ext`/`LinearMap.ext`
  to an element `r`, then `simp [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η, unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply,
  Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp]` (or unfold `restrictScalarsLaxε`). Both sides reduce to `φ.hom.app X r`.
  This step has NO opaque unit, so it is genuinely one-shot.
- **Gap**: identical — every lemma exists; `epsilonPresheafToSheafUnit` is a thin project wrapper
  provable in a few sectionwise lines. NOT a Mathlib gap.
- **Verdict**: PROCEED.

## Recommendation
Author `epsilonPresheafToSheafUnit` as a sectionwise `ext` (decision iii idiom) and close `(∗∗)` with
the documented 3-step recipe, applying these three guard-rails that the prior five passes lacked:
1. **(i)** is `simp only [Functor.comp_map, SheafOfModules.forget_map, SheafOfModules.pushforward_map_val,
   SheafOfModules.comp_val, ← Functor.map_comp]` — named simp lemmas, not a rfl chase.
2. **(ii)** = `(sheafificationAdjunction _).unit.naturality (η F)` then `right_triangle_components`;
   for the single reassociation, do NOT `rw [Category.assoc]` — either reuse the L1714 shape
   `(Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans (h ▸ Category.id_comp _)` or `simp only [Category.assoc]`, or
   open the block with a `show` into the syntactic `PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ …)`
   category so plain `rw` works thereafter.
3. **(iii)** is a clean sectionwise `ext` via `ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η` +
   `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply` + the `Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp` `ε`-formula.
Do not search for a single coherence lemma that also folds (i)+(ii): the one that handles the hard
(opaque-unit) part — `Adjunction.unit_app_unit_comp_map_η` — is already the closed
`presheafUnit_comp_map_eta`, and the remainder is irreducible `.val` bookkeeping with the cheap
idioms above. The friction was always `rw [Category.assoc]` on a defeq-not-syntactic category, not an
API absence.
